question: What business-continuity and confidential-information-access risks does Apple disclose in its FY2024 10-K relating to system failures, vendor breaches, and unauthorized access to personal information?
rationale: Multi-aspect (RESEARCH §7 failure mode C) — the question stresses BOTH business-continuity-disruption AND confidential-information-loss AND third-party-vendor exposure. Many AAPL Item 1A chunks discuss cybersecurity, supply-chain, or business continuity in isolation; the gold chunk is the one that ties failed disaster-recovery to confidential-information loss with the vendor/global-supplier ransomware framing. Re-curated 2026-05-16 (workflow_dispatch run 25948439315) — the prior gold chunk AAPL-FY2024-Item-1A-007 was 439 BGE tokens (~500 XLM-RoBERTa tokens), tripping the bge-reranker-base 512-token silent-truncation per Phase 5 A1 REFUTED finding. New gold n_tokens=390 — safely under the reranker cap.

question: What products and services does Microsoft identify as comprising its Intelligent Cloud segment in FY2024, and how does Azure fit into that segment's revenue mix?
rationale: Near-duplicate boilerplate (RESEARCH §7 failure mode B) — Microsoft mentions Azure in many Item 1 chunks (AI Copilot section, OpenAI partnership section, segment-overview boilerplate). The gold chunk is the specific Intelligent Cloud segment-definition paragraph enumerating Server Products, Enterprise/Partner Services, and Azure's consumption-based revenue model. Reranker should pick this over the Azure-mentions in other chunks. Re-curated 2026-05-16 — prior gold MSFT-FY2024-Item-1-004 was 432 BGE tokens (~492 XLMR, tight against reranker cap). New gold n_tokens=313 — very comfortably under the cap.

question: How does NVIDIA describe the AI Diffusion Interim Final Rule's three-tier country framework, the Universal Verified End User (UVEU) and National Verified End User (NVEU) designations, and their expected impact on its market access?
rationale: Multi-aspect (RESEARCH §7 failure mode C) — the question requires the THREE-TIER framework AND the UVEU/NVEU designation specifics AND the market-impact analysis (retaliation, compliance, supply-chain costs). Lexically similar NVDA Item 1A chunks discuss export controls or trade restrictions broadly, but only this chunk integrates the tier-classification scheme with the UVEU/NVEU labels and the retaliation-risk language. Re-curated 2026-05-16 — prior multi-gold case (NVDA-FY2024-Item-1A-018 + -037, 422 and 442 BGE tokens) had one chunk truncated by the reranker; new single-gold chunk n_tokens=358 is safely under the cap.

question: How does Visa describe the breakdown of its FY2024 net revenue between U.S. and international markets, and what does it identify as the main drivers for service revenue, data processing revenue, and international transaction revenue?
rationale: Multi-aspect (RESEARCH §7 failure mode C) — the question requires U.S.-vs-international breakdown AND the four-component revenue composition (service, data processing, international transaction, other) AND component-specific growth drivers. Many V Item 7 chunks discuss revenue growth or cross-border volume; only this chunk simultaneously addresses the U.S./international split AND the per-component drivers. Re-curated 2026-05-16 — prior gold V-FY2024-Item-7-001 was 420 BGE tokens (~478 XLMR, tight). New gold n_tokens=361 — safely under the cap.

question: How does JPMorgan Chase describe its exposure to commercial real estate lending under sustained adverse economic conditions, including the impact of hybrid work models on office property rental cash flows?
rationale: Multi-aspect (RESEARCH §7 failure mode C) — the question combines economic-downturn risk AND commercial-real-estate (offices) AND hybrid-work-model effects on rental income. Many JPM Item 1A chunks discuss credit risk or economic conditions; only this chunk ties hybrid work to office CRE rental cash flows AND the allowance-for-credit-losses follow-on impact. New case (replaces the prior V cross-border-volume case to diversify ticker coverage). n_tokens=325 — very safe.

question: How did Apple describe the gross-margin trend for its Services segment in FY2024 compared to FY2023?
rationale: Lexical-but-misleading (RESEARCH §7 failure mode A) — many AAPL chunks mention 'Services' (Apple Music, App Store, iCloud pricing pages, Item 1 product overview). The gold chunk is the specific MD&A Services-gross-margin paragraph that attributes the YoY increase to higher Services net sales and a different Services mix — not an Apple Music or App Store product-description chunk. n_tokens=402 — safely under the reranker cap (verified PASSED in workflow_dispatch run 25948439315).

question: How does Procter & Gamble describe its exposure to foreign-currency fluctuations given that non-U.S. operations generate more than half of annual net sales?
rationale: Near-duplicate boilerplate (RESEARCH §7 failure mode B) — every consumer-goods 10-K Item 1A has a generic 'we operate globally and FX rates affect us' paragraph. The gold chunk is the one with the specific FY2024 disclosure that >50% of annual net sales come from non-U.S. markets and that foreign-currency debt and derivatives are part of P&G's capital-markets activities — the quantitative anchor distinguishes this from boilerplate. n_tokens=409 — safe (verified PASSED in workflow_dispatch run 25948439315).

question: How does Home Depot describe the impact of extreme weather conditions and long-term climate change on its inventory mix and supplier-relationship strategy in FY2024?
rationale: Multi-aspect (RESEARCH §7 failure mode C) — the question combines short-term extreme weather AND long-term climate change (physical AND transition risks) AND supplier-quality/availability. Many HD Item 1A chunks discuss supplier risk or weather in isolation; only this chunk ties extreme weather to inventory incompatibility AND climate transition risks to product procurement AND supplier-financial-difficulty consequences. New case (replaces the prior NVDA geopolitical-Taiwan/China case to diversify ticker coverage). n_tokens=319 — very safe.
